ab: muka ku i drekall kirepanauwa kan mulli na mainayan a daman.
ch: 昨天，我去部落拜訪爺爺。
en: Yesterday, I went to visit my grandpa in the tribe.

ab: paetreng mi lla adri marepanau. masupeng ku kantaw.
ch: 我們很久沒有見面了，我很想念他。
en: We haven’t seen each other for a long time and I miss him a lot.

ab: menau i mulli na mainayan kanku i, tu pakanay ku dra taramunag aw tu patrekelay ku dra kulisepa.
ch: 爺爺一看到我，就請我吃西瓜，請我喝茶。
en: As soon as grandpa saw me, he invited me to eat watermelon and drink tea.

ab: dalleu tu sinalem na taramunag kan mulli na mainayan. salaw imaran i!
ch: 爺爺種的西瓜很甜，真的很好吃！
en: The watermelon grown by grandpa is very sweet and really delicious.

ab: sagar ku kantu kulisepa kan mulli na mainayan. imaran i!
ch: 我也喜歡爺爺的茶，也很好喝！
en: I also like grandpa’s tea, it’s tasted good too.

ab: a karealliyan i, karaabel mi dra karealliyan kan mulli na babayan.
ch: 中午的時候，我和奶奶一起煮午餐。
en: At noon, I cooked lunch with my grandma.

ab: marekamenimeni tu inabelan na karealliyan kan mulli na babayan. ullaya a kuraw, a terekuk, a siyak. aw ullaya driya a tamurarang.
ch: 奶奶煮了很豐盛的午餐，有魚、有雞、有南瓜、還有蝸牛。
en: Grandma cooked a big lunch, including fish, chicken, pumpkin, and snails.

ab: peniya mi karealli i, muka mi i bakabak kikulanga.
ch: 吃完午餐後，我們去菜園摘菜。
en: After lunch, we went to the vegetable garden to pick vegetables.

ab: mabak dra sadru dra bunga, dra buir aw dra lugiw. tu pakakasuwan kanku muruma.
ch: 奶奶拿了很多地瓜、芋頭和蕗蕎給我，要我帶回家。
en: Grandma brought me a lot of sweet potatoes, taro and buckwheat, and asked me to take them home.

ab: “uwaburek ku lla. an masall i, dradruwa ku driya kirepanauwa kanemu.” ku kayaw na mulli.
ch: 我跟他們道別:「說下次再去看他們。」
en: I said goodbye to them and said I’d see them next time.
